scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 10 read streams (products, orders, customers, categories, invoices, shipments, creditmemos, customer_groups, store_websites, store_views) and 4 write actions (update_product, create_category, update_category, cancel_order) covering the practically syncable/mutable catalog+sales+store-config surface reachable through the generic `searchCriteria` list convention and simple id-scoped mutations this dialect can express. Excluded: quote/cart checkout-flow endpoints (carts, cart items, shipping estimation, coupon-on-cart) which model an in-progress, session-scoped shopping cart rather than a syncable catalog/sales object and whose write shapes are transactional multi-step checkout sequences, not single-record mutations; Multi-Source Inventory (inventory sources/stocks/source-items), an Adobe Commerce-edition-only module not present on every Magento Open Source install this connector targets; CMS pages/blocks, a content-authoring surface distinct from the catalog/sales domain this connector otherwise covers; binary/multipart product-media-gallery image upload; compound order/invoice/shipment/credit-memo creation endpoints whose real payload is a per-line-item quantity/amount breakdown this dialect's flat single-record body construction cannot safely express; and destructive-admin/elevated-scope endpoints (product/category/customer delete, tax/sales-rule configuration) this connector deliberately does not allow-list as reverse-ETL writes. Paths below are relative to `/rest/<store_code>` (this bundle's `base_url` already includes the `/rest/V1` version prefix; `streams.json`'s stream paths therefore start at the resource segment).

excluded.reason: single-product detail; identical record shape to the products list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

product creation requires a full attribute-set-aware payload (custom attributes, type-specific extension attributes) this dialect's flat body construction cannot safely express without risking a malformed catalog entity; update_product covers the practical mutation case
no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; product deletion is irreversible and out of scope
product media-gallery sub-resource; a nested list keyed off an already-covered product, not modeled as its own top-level stream
product image upload requires a base64-encoded binary media payload; this dialect's write actions send structured JSON/form bodies, not binary asset uploads
single stock-item lookup keyed off a product+item id pair with no list endpoint to enumerate from; not a syncable stream
legacy (pre-MSI) single-source stock-quantity mutation; superseded by the MSI source-items surface, itself excluded this pass as a Commerce-edition-only module
single-category detail; identical record shape to the categories list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; category deletion is irreversible and out of scope
category-to-product assignment sub-list; a nested join-table read, not a top-level syncable stream
category-to-product assignment mutation; a join-table write rather than a mutation of the category or product record itself
single-order detail; identical record shape to the orders list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
order lifecycle state-transition action beyond the single cancel mutation modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
order lifecycle state-transition action beyond the single cancel mutation modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
raw full-order-object creation bypasses the checkout/cart pipeline Magento itself expects orders to be created through; not a safe reverse-ETL write
order status-history comment append; a narrow annotation write, not a full-record mutation, deferred this pass
order status-history sub-resource nested under an already-covered order; not modeled as its own stream
single-customer detail; identical record shape to the customers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
customer creation touches account-credential/password-reset flows this connector does not model as a safe reverse-ETL write
customer profile mutation can alter login credentials and group/tax assignment; treated as elevated-scope admin action, not an allow-listed write
no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; customer deletion is irreversible and out of scope
customer address-book sub-resource nested under an already-covered customer; not modeled as its own top-level stream
single-customer-group detail; identical record shape to the customer_groups list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
customer group creation controls tax-class and permission assignment account-wide; treated as elevated-scope admin configuration, not an allow-listed write
single-invoice detail; identical record shape to the invoices list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
invoice creation is a multi-step order-billing action (partial qty selection, capture-vs-offline) not expressible as this dialect's flat single-record write; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
invoice comment-append annotation write; narrow scope deferred this pass
triggers an outbound customer-facing invoice email; a notification side-effect, not a data mutation
invoice void lifecycle action beyond the mutations modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-shipment detail; identical record shape to the shipments list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
shipment creation requires per-order-item quantity selection plus carrier/tracking-number sub-objects, a compound multi-part payload this dialect's flat body construction cannot safely express
adds a tracking-number sub-object to an existing shipment; a nested-object append write, not a full-record mutation
shipment comment-append annotation write; narrow scope deferred this pass
triggers an outbound customer-facing shipment email; a notification side-effect, not a data mutation
single-credit-memo detail; identical record shape to the creditmemos list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
credit memo creation requires a per-order-item refund-quantity/amount breakdown, a compound payload this dialect's flat body construction cannot safely express
credit memo comment-append annotation write; narrow scope deferred this pass
triggers an outbound customer-facing credit-memo email; a notification side-effect, not a data mutation
per-store runtime configuration snapshot (locale, currency, base URLs), not a syncable business-object stream
the intermediate website-to-store-view grouping layer; both covered endpoints (store_websites/store_views) already surface the same hierarchy without this connecting layer
an active shopping-cart/quote is a session-scoped, in-progress checkout object, not a settled syncable catalog/sales record; the whole quote/cart resource group is out of scope
cart creation is the first step of a multi-request checkout sequence (create cart -> add items -> set addresses -> place order), not a standalone single-record write this dialect models
cart line-item sub-list of an out-of-scope session-scoped cart resource
cart line-item mutation of an out-of-scope session-scoped cart resource
coupon-to-cart application on an out-of-scope session-scoped cart resource
computed cart-total snapshot of an out-of-scope session-scoped cart resource, not a persisted record
the terminal place-order step of the multi-request checkout sequence; the resulting order is already covered by the orders stream once settled
Multi-Source Inventory (MSI) is an Adobe Commerce-edition-only module not present on every Magento Open Source install this connector targets; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
MSI stocks surface; same edition-availability caveat as inventory/sources
MSI source-items surface; same edition-availability caveat as inventory/sources
CMS page content authoring is a distinct content-management domain from the catalog/sales/store-config surface this connector covers; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
CMS block content authoring; same content-management-domain exclusion as cmsPage
cart price rules (promotions) are merchandising configuration, not a transactional catalog/sales record; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
coupon codes are sub-objects of the out-of-scope sales-rule/promotion configuration surface
bulk coupon-code generation returns an async-style batch result, not a single synchronous record; part of the excluded promotions surface
tax-rate configuration affects storefront pricing/compliance account-wide; treated as elevated-scope admin configuration, not an allow-listed reverse-ETL write
single-tax-rate lookup with no list-all endpoint to enumerate from without a search-criteria filter target; narrow admin-configuration read, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
tax-rule configuration affects storefront pricing/compliance account-wide; treated as elevated-scope admin configuration, not an allow-listed reverse-ETL write
tax-class configuration lookup; narrow admin-configuration read, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
related/up-sell/cross-sell product-link sub-resource nested under an already-covered product; not modeled as its own top-level stream
async bulk-operation job-status polling handle, not a syncable business-object stream
admin credential token-issuance endpoint; this connector authenticates with a pre-provisioned long-lived Integration Access Token instead, never performing its own login flow
